intro.title:
TRP & PRP in Europe for those who want to live
and work <highlight>abroad</highlight>
intro.text: Operating since 2018. We guide clients from the initial consultation to obtaining their status—in 10 countries, with local lawyers in each.
intro.info: 147 residence permits obtained in 10 jurisdictions since 2018

possibilities.title: Ten Jurisdictions. One Case Handling Standard.
possibilities.additionalInfo: A local licensed lawyer operates in every country. We select the jurisdiction based on your goals, income, and family composition.

spain.title: Spain
spain.importantText: Digital Nomad Visa — 3-year card right away
spain.ourExperience: We assist from apostille to TIE card. We prepare the contract and the dossier so that the consul has no questions.
spain.hook: Schengen residence permit for 3 years without investments or starting a business. Family applies together with the main applicant.

montenegro.title: Montenegro
montenegro.importantText: Residency via company registration or employment
montenegro.ourExperience: We have been working with the Montenegrin direction since 2018. We register the company, open a bank account, and manage the case until the card is received.
montenegro.hook: A working path to residency without purchasing real estate. Turnkey company, bank account, and residence permit in a single process.

serbia.title: Serbia
serbia.importantText: Residency via establishing a legal entity
serbia.ourExperience: We help with address registration, opening a bank account, and processing the residence permit. We know the local specifics of each district.
serbia.hook: Card valid for up to 3 years right away, family members join via reunification. Minimal physical presence requirements.

france.title: France
france.importantText: Visitor / Business / Startup Visa — up to 4 years
france.ourExperience: We prepare the dossier to meet prefecture requirements. We control the deadlines and provide guidance at all stages.
france.hook: A path to the EU without real estate investments. Suitable for financially independent individuals, entrepreneurs, and startups.

hungary.title: Hungary
hungary.importantText: National Card via establishing a legal entity
hungary.ourExperience: We register the company, open a bank account, and issue the card in a single interconnected process.
hungary.hook: 2-year card with renewal options. Fast entry to the EU through your own company with a local bank account.

malta.title: Malta
malta.ourExperience: We work with licensed Maltese agents. Full support along the naturalization track.
malta.hook: EU citizenship through naturalization via the investment track. The passport opens access to 185 countries.

greece.title: Greece
greece.importantText: Golden Visa via real estate investments
greece.ourExperience: We coordinate the legal support of the transaction and the submission of residence permit documents in a single process.
greece.hook: 5-year card right away. No physical presence requirements — the status remains valid under any schedule.

cyprus.title: Cyprus
cyprus.importantText: Permanent residency by investment — lifelong status
cyprus.ourExperience: We help structure assets for Cypriot residency. We work with local lawyers and banks.
cyprus.hook: Lifelong permanent residency without renewals. Low tax burden and an English-speaking environment.

uae.title: UAE
uae.importantText: Remote Visa / Golden Visa / Freelance Visa
uae.ourExperience: We process visas in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, including Emirates ID and bank account opening. Suitable for investors and founders.
uae.hook: Zero income tax and residency for up to 10 years. The fastest processing framework among all directions.

armenia.title: Armenia
armenia.importantText: Residency via Sole Proprietorship / LLC registration
armenia.ourExperience: We open the company and process the residence permit in a single trip. We know the specifics of banking compliance in Yerevan.
armenia.hook: A quick start for entrepreneurs and IT specialists. Residence permit without real estate investments.

process.title: Three Stages. From Profile Assessment to Residence Card.

step_1.title: Diagnostic Consultation
step_1.text: We analyze your situation: income, citizenship, family composition, goals. We suggest suitable programs with realistic timelines and costs.

step_2.title: Document Preparation
step_2.text: We collect and verify the document package. We control every stage — from translation to final submission.

step_3.title: Obtaining the Status
step_3.text: We guide you until you receive your residence card. Afterwards, we help open a bank account and resolve administrative matters on-site.

aboutUs.title:
Operating in these countries
since 2018.
aboutUs.text:
The Aurion team guides clients across Montenegro, Serbia, Spain, and other destinations.
We take on a limited number of cases at a time — ensuring each is managed by a dedicated specialist, not a queue.

team.title: On-site team since 2018
team.text: We manage cases from the inside, not remotely — this shortens timeframes and eliminates submission risks.

specialist.title: One specialist — one case
specialist.text: Your case is managed by a single manager from the first consultation to the final document acquisition.

continious.title: Post-approval support
continious.text: We help with bank accounts, tax status, and adaptation. We stay in touch after you receive your card.

cases.title: Cases brought to the final residence card status.

montenegroBusiness.outcome: Residence permit for a family of four under a single ground.
montenegroBusiness.description: An applicant with passive income obtained a residence permit for himself, his spouse, and two children within one submission framework.

spainNomad.outcome: Digital Nomad Visa for a remote specialist.
spainNomad.description: An IT specialist verified income from foreign contracts and obtained a Schengen residence permit with the right to reside in the EU.

UAE.outcome: 10-year Golden Visa for an IT company founder
UAE.description: The founder processed a long-term residence permit along with an Emirates ID and a corporate account without leaving the country.

finalCta.title: Program conditions change quarterly. Submitting today means dealing with yesterday's requirements.
finalCta.text: During a 30-minute consultation, we will assess your profile and provide realistic timeframes for your jurisdiction.

footer.description: AI platform for assessment, forecasting, and comparison of land and real estate value in international markets.

roadmap.title: How the process unfolds — week by week.
roadmap.additionalInfo: Timelines are specified for a typical real estate case in Montenegro.

steps.title: Consultation and Assessment
steps.text: We study your profile, source of income, and family composition. We verify the grounds and establish a realistic schedule.

steps.title: Collection and Preparation of Documents
steps.text: We collect the set, arrange apostilles, sworn translations, and notarization. Every document is verified before submission.

steps.title: Application Submission
steps.text: We submit the case to the authorized body in Montenegro. We accompany the submission in person — eliminating remote risks.

steps.title: Review by Authorities
steps.text: We track the application status and respond to requests from the authority. You receive updates at every step.

steps.title: Receiving the Card and Settlement
steps.text: You receive your plastic residence permit card. We help with a bank account, tax number, and the first renewal.

overview.title: A single status that unlocks residency, a bank account, and freedom of movement across the Balkans.
overview.firstText: A residence permit based on passive income is a temporary residence permit in Montenegro for those who receive regular income outside the country: dividends, rental payments, interest on deposits, or remote compensation. Real estate purchase is not required.
overview.secondText: We manage the case from the initial consultation to receiving the plastic residence card: we select the grounds, prepare and certify documents, submit the application, and provide support post-approval. The status is renewed annually and grants the right to permanent residency after five years.

tariffs.title: Transparent Rates Without Hidden Fees
tariffs.additionalText: Cost of Aurion services. State fees, translations, and notary services are paid separately and specified in advance.
tariffs.PS: The indicated cost is for support services. State fees, apostille, translations, notary, and insurance are paid separately and agreed upon before work begins.

essential.title: Basic
essential.additional: For those who apply independently and want a verified strategy.

full.title: Full Support
full.additional: Turnkey case from the first consultation to the residence card for a single applicant.

family.title: Family
family.additional: Full support for a family of up to four people within a single application.

points.point2: Residence permits for spouse and children
points.point3: Coordination of the family document set
points.point4: Support with schools and bank accounts

requirements.title: Who qualifies and what is required.

age.title: Age 18+
age.text: The application is submitted by an adult. Children are included as dependents.

cleanRecord.title: Clean Criminal Record
cleanRecord.text: A certificate of no criminal record from the country of citizenship and country of residence.

passport.title: Valid Passport
passport.text: Validity period of no less than six months on the date of submission.

entry.title: No Entry Bans
entry.text: No active restrictions on entry to Montenegro or the Schengen zone.

spouse.title: Spouse
spouse.text: Included in the case provided there is a marriage certificate with an apostille.

children.title: Minor Children
children.text: Children under 18 are processed as dependents of the main applicant.

students.title: Student Children
students.text: Adult children dependent upon verification of active enrollment.

financeCondition.title: Financial Condition per Person
financeCondition.text: Verified income is calculated for all included family members.

income.title: Verified Passive Income
income.text: Dividends, rent, interest, or remote income — from €3,650 per year.

statement.title: Bank Statement
statement.text: Funds movement over the last 12 months on a personal account.

housing.title: Housing in the Country
housing.text: A lease agreement or ownership rights for the residential address.

insurance.title: Medical Insurance
insurance.text: A policy covering the validity period of the requested residence permit.

cleanRecordDoc.title: Criminal Record Certificate
cleanRecordDoc.text: With an apostille, issued no earlier than six months prior to submission.

civilDocs.title: Civil Certificates
civilDocs.text: Marriage and birth certificates for family applications.

notary.title: Translation and Notarization
notary.text: Sworn translation into the Montenegrin language and notary certification.

incomeProof.title: Income Verification
incomeProof.text: Documents regarding the source of funds and bank reference letters.

property.title: Is it necessary to buy real estate of a certain value for this residence permit?
property.text: No, Montenegrin legislation does not establish a minimum value threshold for the property. The primary condition is that the property must be residential (apartment, house) or commercial and belong to you by right of ownership. Shared ownership stakes are also eligible for processing.

duration.title: For what period is the residence permit issued and how is it renewed?
duration.text: A residence permit (boravak) based on real estate is issued for exactly 1 year. It can be renewed annually an unlimited number of times as long as the property remains in your ownership. The renewal procedure is simpler than the initial submission but requires updating local certificates and insurance.

taxes.title: What additional expenses and taxes will arise during the purchase?
taxes.text: When buying secondary real estate, a turnover tax of 3% to 5% is paid depending on the property's value (primary properties from a developer who is a VAT payer are exempt from this tax). Also account for the costs of notary services, a court translator, and the annual real estate tax.

permanent.title: Does this type of residence permit lead to permanent residency and citizenship?
permanent.text: According to the current Law on Foreigners of Montenegro, a residence permit based on property ownership is temporary and does not directly count toward the 5-year residency history required to obtain permanent residency (stalni boravak) or citizenship. A residence permit via company formation is typically used for these purposes.

residencyTime.title: Is it mandatory to stay in Montenegro continuously after receiving the residence permit?
residencyTime.text: To maintain a residence permit based on real estate, there are currently no strict requirements for a mandatory 11-month physical presence in the country (unlike a work-based residence permit). However, prolonged absence may raise additional questions during the annual card renewal.
